key_findings: Recommended stack: FastAPI (API) + React 18 + Vite 5 (frontend) — Python API reuses controller ecosystem; React provides mature table/graph/chart components needed by WO-00012 IA
Additional monthly ops cost for Phase 1–2: $0 — FastAPI on controller host, React static build served by nginx alongside existing services
Phase 3 VPS split (optional): $4–20/mo on Hetzner CX21/CX32 if api_p95_latency_ms ORS signal triggers
TanStack Table v8 chosen for analyst tables — virtualised rows, server-side sort/filter, React-native; Svelte equivalent is community-maintained and less featured
Cytoscape.js (graph view) must be dynamically imported (code-split) — 300KB library only loaded on Attack Surface Map route; initial bundle target <150KB
URL-encoded filter state (React Router URLSearchParams) must be implemented day 1 — cannot be retrofitted cheaply into existing component tree
Rollback: API = symlink swap to previous version (<10s downtime); Frontend = nginx symlink swap to previous static build (<0s downtime); no database migration needed
FastAPI + Next.js rejected: SSR adds ops burden (Node.js process) with no benefit for internal non-SEO tool; FastAPI + SvelteKit rejected: smaller ecosystem, less mature table/graph libraries
Neo4j self-hosted retained: Aura Professional costs $65/mo with no operational benefit for internal tooling at current scale
ORS trigger for VPS split: api_p95_latency_ms > 2000 for 3 consecutive minutes — explicit signal prevents premature infrastructure cost

decisions.title: FastAPI + React/Vite over FastAPI + Next.js or SvelteKit
decisions.summary: Python API (FastAPI) eliminates language mismatch with controller; React ecosystem provides mature TanStack Table/Query and Cytoscape.js; zero additional infra cost in Phase 1–2
decisions.rationale: SSR (Next.js/SvelteKit) provides no benefit for internal non-SEO tooling. SvelteKit ecosystem lacks mature table and graph components needed by the 8-view IA design. FastAPI reuses the same neo4j-driver and clickhouse-connect packages already tested in the controller codebase.

decisions.title: Deployment on controller host (Phase 1–2), VPS split deferred (Phase 3+)
decisions.summary: FastAPI (systemd/Uvicorn) co-located with controller; nginx serves static React build and proxies /api/*; zero additional monthly cost
decisions.rationale: Phase 1–2 analyst query load (2–5 concurrent analysts) does not justify separate VPS. Controller host headroom is sufficient. VPS split triggered by ORS signal, not by schedule.

decisions.title: TanStack Query v5 for all API calls including 30s Operations Log polling
decisions.summary: TanStack Query handles server state, cache invalidation, background refetch intervals, and loading/error states; no additional state management library needed
decisions.rationale: Operations Log requires 30s auto-refresh. TanStack Query's refetchInterval option handles this with zero custom code, automatic background refetch, and stale-while-revalidate semantics. Replaces Redux/Zustand for server state.

decisions.title: Cytoscape.js dynamically imported (code-split)
decisions.summary: Graph library (~300KB) loaded only when Attack Surface Map route is activated; dynamic import at Phase 3; prevents initial bundle bloat
decisions.rationale: Phase 1–2 analysts never load the graph view. Including Cytoscape.js in the initial bundle would double the JS payload unnecessarily. React.lazy() + Suspense enables clean code splitting with loading fallback.

decisions.title: Neo4j self-hosted retained; Aura Professional rejected
decisions.summary: Aura Professional costs $65/mo with no operational benefit for internal tooling at current 10M+ subdomain scale
decisions.rationale: Self-hosted Neo4j already operational, already tuned for KRIL/dedup workloads. Aura would add $65/mo for managed hosting with no latency or reliability improvement on internal LAN. Migration risk exceeds benefit.

decisions.title: CSV export: client-side from JSON response; PDF export: browser print dialog
decisions.summary: No server-side PDF generation library (wkhtmltopdf, WeasyPrint) — uses @media print CSS; CSV generated client-side with papaparse + FileSaver
decisions.rationale: Server-side PDF adds ops complexity (headless Chrome, wkhtmltopdf system dep). Browser print-to-PDF is sufficient for Executive Summary export. CSV is a direct JSON → CSV transform, trivially done client-side.

database_drivers.notes: official async driver, built-in connection pool

libraries.name: recharts 2.x
libraries.purpose: throughput area chart, severity bar chart

libraries.name: Tailwind CSS 3.x
libraries.purpose: utility-first CSS, zero design system overhead

libraries.name: papaparse + FileSaver
libraries.purpose: client-side CSV export from API JSON

reverse_proxy.tls: optional; HTTP sufficient for LAN-only v1

candidate_matrix.name: FastAPI + Next.js
candidate_matrix.rejection_reason: SSR unnecessary for internal tool; Node.js adds second language/process; $30-60/mo vs $0 with no benefit

candidate_matrix.name: FastAPI + SvelteKit
candidate_matrix.rejection_reason: Smaller ecosystem; TanStack Table not natively supported; Cytoscape.js Svelte adapter immature; team familiarity cost

api_endpoints.notes: full detail: IPs, endpoints, findings, CNAME chain, technologies
same filter params as /findings; streams CSV content-type
time-series for Operations Log throughput panel

deployment_options.name: Separate VPS
deployment_options.network: internal VPN to Neo4j + ClickHouse on controller host

cost_model.neo4j_aura_professional_rejected: $65/mo (no benefit over self-hosted for internal tool)

api.procedure: systemctl stop recon-api; ln -sfn /opt/recon-api-versions/<prev> /opt/recon-api; systemctl start recon-api

database.api_writes: read-only for Phase 1–2; no migration needed
database.property_changes: update FastAPI query layer only; no data migration
database.clickhouse: additive materialized views only; deprecated after validation

blue_green.procedure: Run new API on :8001; smoke test; nginx upstream swap; stop :8000

implementation_phases.name: Operations Log + Asset Discovery + Asset Detail
Finding Triage + Template Detail + Executive Summary
Attack Surface Map + HTTP Endpoint Browser
frontend_deliverables: Graph view (500 node cap, KRIL-sized nodes)
HTTP Endpoint Browser with preset filters

risk_model.title: FastAPI latency under concurrent analyst load
risk_model.mitigation: Async neo4j driver + pool_size=50; ClickHouse materialized views for aggregates; ORS signal api_p95_latency_ms

risk_model.title: Cytoscape.js bundle bloat in initial load
risk_model.mitigation: Dynamic import (React.lazy); only loaded on /graph route; initial bundle target <150KB

risk_model.title: TanStack Table breaks on 50k+ unvirtualised rows
risk_model.mitigation: API pagination (page_size=100); virtualised row renderer for in-memory data

risk_model.title: React scope creep before Phase 1 ships
risk_model.mitigation: Phase gate enforcement: no graph imports until Phase 3; no additional views before Phase 1 complete

risk_model.title: nginx SPA routing breaks on direct URL access
ClickHouse FINAL query latency on high-ingest period
risk_model.mitigation: API queries materialized view not raw table; 30s refresh SLO acceptable

risk_model.title: VPS cost trigger not defined
risk_model.mitigation: ORS signal api_p95_latency_ms > 2000 for 3 consecutive minutes triggers VPS split evaluation

recommended_actions.action: Scaffold FastAPI with /api/v1/stats/summary and /api/v1/subdomains first — enables Operations Log and Asset Discovery (Phase 1)
Create systemd unit and nginx config (SPA fallback + API proxy) before writing first React component
Implement URL-encoded filter state (React Router URLSearchParams) from day 1 — cannot retrofit cheaply
Use TanStack Query refetchInterval for Operations Log 30s polling — zero custom code
Set up Vite dynamic import scaffold for Cytoscape.js from Phase 1 — prevents bundle bloat at Phase 3
Add ORS signal api_p95_latency_ms — triggers VPS split if controller host saturates
Retain last 3 frontend builds in versioned directories — enables 0s rollback via nginx symlink swap
Use ClickHouse materialized views for all /api/v1/stats/* — required for ≤1s filter SLO

assumptions: A1: Controller host has sufficient CPU headroom for 4 Uvicorn workers alongside existing processes
A2: neo4j-driver 5.x async session compatible with existing Neo4j version on controller
A3: ClickHouse materialized views exist for stats aggregates (required by WO-00012 filter performance targets)
A4: Internal LAN access only for v1; HTTP without TLS acceptable; nginx TLS optional if needed
A5: npm and Python 3.12 build toolchain available on controller or CI for deployments
A6: Authentication deferred to v1.1 (WO-00012 A5); no JWT/session middleware in Phase 1
A7: Python 3.11+ on controller host (FastAPI + Pydantic v2 requirement)
